insight:
Yesterday was a solid training session with your 12.6km run completed at a controlled aerobic pace - that 148 bpm average heart rate shows great restraint just 7 days out from Chester. Your excellent step count of 17,645 and strong calorie burn of 3,320 kcal demonstrate you're staying active while managing intensity. However, that 4.3 hours of sleep is concerning this close to race day.

Your HRV of 102ms sits perfectly within your baseline range and your resting heart rate of 48 bpm shows solid cardiovascular fitness. The low stress score of 35/100 confirms your body handled yesterday's session well. These recovery markers suggest you can proceed with today's planned session without modifications - your body is responding positively to the training load.

Given yesterday's massive calorie deficit and minimal sleep, prioritize getting 7-9 hours of quality sleep tonight and aim to eat closer to 2,800-3,000 calories today to properly fuel your recovery and race preparation.
